typecheck_backend.details: 99 errors in 15 files — pre-existing type errors scheduled for fix in milestone 'deployment' (feature deploy-mypy-fixes). Not introduced by benchmark-mode.

typecheck_frontend.details: 2 errors in BenchmarkPage.test.tsx: (1) TS2769 at line 243 — getByText callback returns boolean|undefined, (2) TS2322 at line 611 — null not assignable to number in trust_scores Record value. Both introduced by fix-benchmark-lint-and-tests commit.

lint_frontend.details: 0 errors, 12 warnings (all pre-existing: complexity, max-lines, react-hooks/exhaustive-deps)

validatorNote: Round 2 after fix-benchmark-lint-and-tests (commit 75551d4). All 5 original blocking issues from round 1 are resolved: unused imports removed, test selector ambiguity fixed, HTMLInputElement globals added, unused function removed, score=0 bug fixed. However, the fix introduced 2 new TypeScript errors in BenchmarkPage.test.tsx. Backend tests (303/303) and frontend tests (212/212) all pass. ESLint clean (0 errors). Backend mypy still has 99 pre-existing errors scheduled for deployment milestone. Frontend typecheck fails with 2 new errors introduced by the fix.

blockingIssues.description: TypeScript error TS2769: BenchmarkPage.test.tsx line 243 — getByText callback returns boolean|undefined instead of boolean. The && chain with optional chaining (element?.classList?.contains()) short-circuits to undefined when element is null. Fix: wrap in !!() or add explicit boolean return.

TypeScript error TS2322: BenchmarkPage.test.tsx line 611 — null assigned to Record<string, number> value in trust_scores. Benchmark.trust_scores type is Record<string, number> | null (whole object nullable, not individual values). Fix: update Benchmark type to Record<string, number | null> | null to correctly model per-URL null scores for failed audits, and update backend BenchmarkResponse schema to match.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'Workers MUST actually run all verification commands listed in their handoff and report ACCURATE exit codes and output. False verification claims (e.g., claiming tsc passes when it has errors) are a blocking trust issue. If a verification step fails, report it honestly — do not claim success.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-benchmark-lint-and-tests handoff claims 'npx tsc -b' exitCode 0 with 'TypeScript strict check passes with no errors', but tsc actually produces 2 errors (TS2769 and TS2322). This is the second round where inaccurate verification claims were made (round 1 had similar ESLint false claims). This pattern is systemic across multiple workers in this milestone.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'When fixing type-related bugs (e.g., changing null handling), workers must verify that the fix is consistent with TypeScript types. If runtime behavior expects null but the type definition uses Record<string, T>, the type definition must be updated to Record<string, T | null> to match actual data shapes from the backend.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-benchmark-lint-and-tests changed `!score` to `score === null` in BenchmarkPage.tsx to handle failed audit null scores, but didn't update the Benchmark type's trust_scores from `Record<string, number> | null` to `Record<string, number | null> | null`. The test then used null as a per-URL score value, causing TS2322.

rejectedObservations.observation: Extra blank lines after removing dead code in BenchmarkPage.tsx and SubmitPage.tsx
rejectedObservations.reason: Minor cosmetic issue with no functional impact. Removing blank lines is not worth a dedicated fix feature.

rejectedObservations.observation: The Benchmark type trust_scores inconsistency between type definition and runtime behavior
rejectedObservations.reason: Already captured as a blocking issue fix requirement — the type needs updating. Not a separate observation.
